reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: My own answer to 4×19 is solid, but Q2–Q4 are all unverified, so volunteering now would risk my life on three unchecked answers. Verifying instead of passing doesn’t change my death risk this round (random death only happens if nobody volunteers) but does cost a coin. In the last two games, B has reliably volunteered in round 1 and been correct, so it’s likely someone volunteers again; I’ll conserve coins and wait rather than take the early-leader risk myself.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (87 - 39) is simple, so I’m certain my capsule Q2-B is correct. All other puzzles in previous games have been basic arithmetic with no wrong answers observed, and there are no conflicting capsules for any question this round. Verifying would only burn coins and still leave the risk of random death if no one volunteers, as happened in Game 1. Volunteering immediately both avoids that random-death risk and, given the simplicity of all questions, is very likely to let everyone escape while I keep all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: In Round 1, every question only has a single-endorsed capsule, so volunteering would mean staking my life on three unverified answers from others—too risky when I gain nothing extra by going first.  

If no one volunteers, a random player dies regardless of whether I pass or verify, so verifying doesn’t reduce my survival risk this round; it just costs me 1 coin. From previous games, B has repeatedly chosen to volunteer in Round 1 and succeeded, so there’s a high chance someone else will step up and prevent the random elimination while I keep my full 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
